meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to track bugs in real-time using Antigravity and Claude Code for your next project.
content.answer_block: Debugging blind is the primary cause of student burnout. By integrating event-based analytics, you transform 'unknown crashes' into actionable data streams. This setup allows you to identify exactly where your logic fails, correlate errors with user state, and fix bugs before your users even realize something went wrong.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the deployment failed, and the error log is an indecipherable wall of text. You’ve spent three hours chasing a ghost in your codebase, and the frustration is starting to outweigh the thrill of building. This isn't a lack of talent—it's a lack of visibility.

Professional engineers don't guess; they instrument. By shifting from reactive debugging to proactive observability, you gain a superpower that separates hobbyists from architects. We are going to wire your application with surgical precision, turning every runtime error into a clear, documented path to a resolution.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Event Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define what 'failure' looks like in your app. Map out user flows and identify the entry/exit points where state is most likely to corrupt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and generate a JSON schema for tracking user interaction events and error states. Focus on identifying critical failure points in the authentication and data submission flows.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze current codebase and suggest 5 critical observability points for telemetry'.

steps.title: Inject Telemetry Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized wrapper that captures every error event and pushes it to your analytics provider without bloating your core business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, reusable TypeScript middleware function that catches global errors, extracts the stack trace, and sends a structured event payload to the analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a centralized error-handling middleware and apply it to the main application entry point'.

steps.title: Implement Context-Aware Logging
steps.description: Errors are useless without state. Add user-session context to your logs to differentiate between edge cases and widespread bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update my error logging function to automatically append the current user's session ID, route, and local state object to every telemetry event sent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Refactor error logging to include session context and local state snapshots'.

steps.title: Automate Failure Alerts
steps.description: Configure your analytics dashboard to ping you when a specific error threshold is crossed, ensuring you never miss a regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a configuration file for my analytics setup that defines threshold-based alerts for 400 and 500 series errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create alert configuration files for production error monitoring'.

steps.title: Analyze and Iterate
steps.description: Use the data collected to perform a 'post-mortem' on your own code, using Claude Code to refactor the identified trouble spots.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the last 24 hours of error logs from my analytics dashboard and suggest code refactoring strategies for the top 3 recurring issues.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Review recent error logs and refactor the problematic modules identified'.

pro_tips: Log the 'before' state: Always capture the user's input variables immediately before a function call to reproduce bugs locally.
Use unique event IDs: Generate a UUID for every error event so you can trace a single user's journey across multiple microservices.
Implement 'Silent Failures': Track errors that don't crash the app but return unexpected results—these are your biggest technical debt indicators.
Throttle your logs: In high-traffic apps, use sampling to avoid hitting API rate limits or incurring unnecessary costs.
Correlate with deployments: Tag every log event with your current Git commit hash to instantly see if a new feature introduced the bug.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, set a weekly 'Review Day' where you analyze the logs for 30 minutes. Use these insights to prioritize your refactoring backlog instead of guessing what to improve next.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility of a senior engineer. Stop fighting your code and start mastering it with the right instrumentation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
